does: This work presents a structured roadmap for developing AI systems that prioritize child safety and welfare. The blueprint outlines concrete safeguards, design principles, and operational practices aimed at protecting minors while ensuring age-appropriate interactions. It integrates technical safety measures with policy frameworks and collaborative approaches, providing a comprehensive strategy for responsible AI deployment in contexts involving children. The document synthesizes best practices for content moderation, privacy protection, developmental appropriateness, and transparent communication with young users and their guardians.
connects: This builds on decades of child protection research in digital environments while extending principles from content moderation and machine learning safety literature to the emerging challenge of generative AI systems. It responds to growing concerns about AI risks to minors documented in child welfare research and addresses gaps left by earlier frameworks focused primarily on adult-oriented harms. The work synthesizes insights from child development psychology, digital rights advocacy, and responsible AI practices pioneered by the broader AI safety community, creating a cohesive framework that bridges technical AI safety with established child protection standards.
enables: This blueprint establishes foundation for industry-wide standards in child-safe AI development, potentially influencing regulatory requirements and corporate policies around AI systems accessible to minors. It enables future research into age-specific model behaviors, adaptive safety mechanisms that adjust to developmental stages, and long-term empirical assessment of safeguarding effectiveness. The framework also facilitates cross-sector collaboration between AI developers, child protection organizations, educators, and families, creating opportunities to develop shared evaluation metrics, incident response protocols, and ongoing governance structures for maintaining child safety as AI capabilities evolve.
